---
title: "clean_taxonomy"
output: html_document
date: "2026-02-04"
---

# clean taxonomy: pull taxa names & build dict of synonyms

## load packages

```{julia}
using CSV, DataFrames
using JLD2
using GeoIO, GeoStats
```

## load in powo checklist

I downloaded a checklist of species in Boryaceae from Kew / Plants of the World Online(POWO) on 2026_06_24 using the settings "Botanical Country", "Native and Introduced taxa", and "Summary Checklist (Names listed by taxonomic status)"

Now, let's creade a vector with all the synonyms from POWO:
```{julia}
lines = readlines("data/taxonomy_trait_data/powochecklist_Boryaceae.txt")
#create a vector called "syn" than contains all synonym listings
syn_pos = findall(contains("==="), lines)
syn = lines[syn_pos]
```

and one with all the accepted names from POWO:
```{julia}
#create a vector called "acc" with all the accepted names
acc = String[]
#Itterate through until we reach a Species header. Then, add everything that comes after it to a vector until we reach a Synonyms header. Do this for the entire checklist. 
for (i,line) in enumerate(lines)
    if contains("$line", "Species")
        for (j,entry) in enumerate(lines[i:end])
            if contains("$entry", "Synonyms")
                deleteat!(acc, lastindex(acc))
                break 
            else 
                push!(acc, lines[j+i])
            end
        end

    end
end

println(acc)
```

Here's a regular expression to pull the full name including the species authority.
```{julia}
#define regex pattern just to pull full name including species authority
pat = Regex(
    "^.+?(?=,)",
    "m"
)
```

Let's create a vector of the accepted scientific names:
```{julia}
AccNames = String[]

for newline in acc
    for a in eachmatch(pat, newline)
        push!(AccNames, "$(a.match)")
    end
end
println(AccNames)
```


Let's initialize a dictionary of accepted names and synonyms:

```{julia}
synonymdict = Dict{String, Vector{Tuple{String, Union{String, Missing}}}}()
for name in AccNames
    synonymdict[name] = Tuple{String, Union{String, Missing}}[]
end
```

## gather my valid names vs. synonyms & their authorities

### get species authorities for valid names

first, get the species authorities for all our valid names (each line in the `blocks` object):
```{julia}
# Create authority dictionary for valid names
authority_dict = Dict{String, String}()

for block in blocks
    first_line = split(block, "\n")[1]
    name_match = match(pat, first_line)
    
    if name_match !== nothing
        name = strip(name_match[1])
        name_parts = split(name, " ")
        epithet = length(name_parts) > 2 ? join(name_parts[2:end], " ") : name_parts[2]
        
        authority_match = match(Regex("\\b$(epithet)\\s+(.+?),"), first_line)
        if authority_match !== nothing
            authority = strip(authority_match[1])
            authority = replace(authority, r"\s+in\s+.*$" => "")
            authority_dict[name] = authority
        end
    end
end

# Add speciesAuthority column to taxa_traits
taxa_traits.speciesAuthority = [get(authority_dict, name, missing) for name in taxa_traits.scientificName]
```

now we have `authority_dict` that takes all valid names (including those we don't have sampled)
to their species authority, and we've updated `taxa_traits`.

### parse all synonym names

then, let's evaluate the synonyms section of the POWO checklist.
we can collect all synonyms and see if there are synonymic epithets
that also match accepted epithets (`duplicated_taxa`)

```{julia}
powosynonyms = lines[1112:end]
synonympat = Regex("^(.+?)\\s+(?=\\(|[A-Z])")

synonym_names = Set{String}()

# collect ALL synonym names (even those we haven't sampled) from entire powosynonyms section
for line in powosynonyms
    if occursin("===", line)
        m = match(synonympat, line)
        if m !== nothing
            synonym = strip(m[1])
            push!(synonym_names, synonym)
        end
    end
end
```

### find epithets that are both accepted & synonyms

```{julia}
# find duplicated taxa (appear as both valid and synonym)
valid_set = Set(validnames)
duplicated_taxa = intersect(valid_set, synonym_names)
```

duplicated_taxa = Set(["Calceolaria perfoliata", "Calceolaria integrifolia", "Calceolaria glandulosa", "Calceolaria biflora", "Calceolaria crenata", "Calceolaria pinnata", "Calceolaria paralia", "Calceolaria hypoleuca", "Calceolaria nudicaulis", "Calceolaria cavanillesii", "Calceolaria nivalis", "Calceolaria brachiata", "Calceolaria deflexa", "Calceolaria ferruginea", "Calceolaria angustiflora", "Calceolaria parvifolia", "Calceolaria heterophylla", "Calceolaria uniflora"])

Note, some of these names may not be valid names that *we have sampled*, but they may be a synonym to
one we have sampled (ex. Calceolaria hypoleuca Kraenzl. is a synonym of Calceolaria soratensis Kraenzl.).
So, let's not filter this list until we map synonyms to their accepted names.

#### make dataframe mapping epithets that are both accepted & synonyms to their authorities

```{julia}
# Initialize vectors for the homonym dataframe
scientificName_vec = String[]
genus_vec = String[]
specificEpithet_vec = String[]
accepted_vec = Bool[]
authority_vec = String[]
validName_vec = Union{String, Missing}[]
synonymAuthority_vec = Union{String, Missing}[]

# Process each duplicated taxon at species level only
for taxon in duplicated_taxa
    # Only process species-level (2 words)
    parts = split(taxon, " ")
    if length(parts) != 2
        continue
    end
    
    genus = parts[1]
    epithet = parts[2]
    
    # Get valid authority from authority_dict (already computed)
    valid_authority = get(authority_dict, taxon, missing)
    
    # Add the valid entry (accepted = TRUE)
    push!(scientificName_vec, taxon)
    push!(genus_vec, genus)
    push!(specificEpithet_vec, epithet)
    push!(accepted_vec, true)
    push!(authority_vec, valid_authority !== missing ? valid_authority : "")
    push!(validName_vec, missing)
    push!(synonymAuthority_vec, missing)
    
    # Find all synonym entries (but only species-level synonyms)
    for line in powosynonyms
        if startswith(line, taxon * " ") && occursin("===", line)
            parts_line = split(line, "===")
            synonym_part = strip(parts_line[1])
            
            # Make sure the synonym itself is species-level (exactly 2 words before the authority)
            # Extract just the name part before the authority
            syn_name_match = match(synonympat, synonym_part)
            if syn_name_match !== nothing
                syn_name = strip(syn_name_match[1])
                syn_name_parts = split(syn_name, " ")
                
                # Skip if not exactly 2 words (genus + species)
                if length(syn_name_parts) != 2
                    continue
                end
            else
                continue
            end
            
            valid_part = strip(parts_line[2])
            
            # Extract synonym authority
            syn_auth_match = match(Regex("\\b$(epithet)\\s+(.+?),"), synonym_part)
            syn_authority = ""
            if syn_auth_match !== nothing
                syn_authority = strip(syn_auth_match[1])
                syn_authority = replace(syn_authority, r"\s+in\s+.*$" => "")
            end
            
            # Extract the valid name it points to
            valid_name_match = match(Regex("^([A-Z][a-z]+\\s+[a-z]+(?:\\s+(?:subsp\\.|var\\.)\\s+[a-z]+)?)"), valid_part)
            valid_name = valid_name_match !== nothing ? strip(valid_name_match[1]) : ""
            valid_name_auth = get(authority_dict, valid_name, missing)
            
            # Add the synonym entry (accepted = FALSE)
            push!(scientificName_vec, taxon)
            push!(genus_vec, genus)
            push!(specificEpithet_vec, epithet)
            push!(accepted_vec, false)
            push!(authority_vec, syn_authority)
            push!(validName_vec, valid_name)
            push!(synonymAuthority_vec, valid_name_auth)
        end
    end
end

# Create the homonym dataframe
homonym_df = DataFrame(
    scientificName = scientificName_vec,
    genus = genus_vec,
    specificEpithet = specificEpithet_vec,
    accepted = accepted_vec,
    authority = authority_vec,
    validName = validName_vec,
    synonymAuthority = synonymAuthority_vec
)
```

### build our synonym dictionary (mapping valid names to synonyms)

```{julia}
for name in scientificName
    valid_auth = get(authority_dict, name, missing)
    if name == "Calceolaria dentata subsp. cummingiana"; continue; end
    synonymdict[name] = [(name, valid_auth)]
end

# Add synonyms to the dictionary for our dataset
for name in scientificName
    keyname = name
    if name == "Calceolaria corymbosa subsp. montana"
        push!(synonymdict[name], ("Calceolaria mollissima", missing))
        name = "Calceolaria mollissima"
    end
    
    # Match === followed by the name, then either whitespace or end of string
    # This works for both "=== Name Author" and "=== Name subsp. infraname"
    validnamepat = Regex("===\\s+\\Q$name\\E(?:\\s|\$)")
    potentialmatches = findall(x -> occursin(validnamepat, x), powosynonyms)
    
    if !isempty(potentialmatches)
        for i in potentialmatches
            line = powosynonyms[i]
            synonym = strip(match(synonympat, line)[1])
            syn_parts = split(synonym, " ")
            syn_epithet = length(syn_parts) > 2 ? join(syn_parts[2:end], " ") : syn_parts[2]
            
            # Escape special regex characters in the epithet
            escaped_epithet = replace(syn_epithet, r"([.*+?^$()[\]{}|\\])" => s"\\\1")
            
            auth_match = match(Regex("\\b$(escaped_epithet)\\s+(.+?),"), line)
            syn_authority = missing
            if auth_match !== nothing
                syn_authority = strip(auth_match[1])
                syn_authority = replace(syn_authority, r"\s+in\s+.*$" => "")
            end
            
            push!(synonymdict[keyname], (synonym, syn_authority))       
        end
    end
end

# preview synonyms
for k in keys(synonymdict)
    if length(synonymdict[k]) > 1
        @show k synonymdict[k]
    end
end
```

### filter homonym_df to only include species of interest

now, we can filter the `homonym_df` to only include values in our synonym dictionary.
```{julia}
synonymdict_values = Set{String}()
for (key, value_list) in synonymdict
    for (syn_name, syn_auth) in value_list
        push!(synonymdict_values, syn_name)
    end
end

homonym_df = filter(homonym_df) do row
    name_match = row.scientificName in synonymdict_values
    valid_match = !ismissing(row.validName) && row.validName in synonymdict_values
    name_match || valid_match
end
```

## gather native ranges from checklist 

```{julia}
# Get the set of taxa in our dataset
dataset_taxa = Set(taxa_traits.scientificName)

# Dictionary to store taxon -> country codes
country_dict = Dict{String, Vector{String}}()

# Pattern to extract taxon name
pat = Regex("^(.+?)\\s+(?=\\(|[A-Z])", "m")

# Pattern to extract 3-letter uppercase country codes
country_pat = r"\b([A-Z]{3})\b"

for block in blocks
    # Extract the taxon name
    name_match = match(pat, block)
    taxon_name = strip(name_match[1])

    # Only process if in our dataset
    # deal with taxa where we're using a different name compared to the valid POWO names
    if taxon_name == "Calceolaria dentata subsp. cumingiana"
        taxon_name = "Calceolaria dentata subsp. cummingiana"
    elseif taxon_name == "Calceolaria mollissima"
        taxon_name = "Calceolaria corymbosa subsp. montana"
    elseif !(taxon_name in dataset_taxa)
        continue
    end
    
    # Find all 3-letter uppercase codes
    country_codes = String[]
    for m in eachmatch(country_pat, block)
        code = m[1]
        push!(country_codes, code)
    end
        
    country_dict[taxon_name] = country_codes
end

# Add to taxa_traits dataframe
taxa_traits.botanicalCountries = [get(country_dict, name, String[]) for name in taxa_traits.scientificName]
```

write out clean version of `taxa_traits`, my homonym df, my synonym dict:
```{julia}
save("data/taxonomy_trait_data/synonymdict.jld2", synonymdict)
CSV.write("data/taxonomy_trait_data/homonyms.csv", homonym_df)
CSV.write("data/taxonomy_trait_data/traits_species_cleaned-2026_02_25.csv", taxa_traits)
```

## consider edge cases

1. check to see if I have anything like one sample C. filicaulis and another C. filicaulis luxurians --> in which case, do you filter out all the luxurians for the former? what makes sense for niche modeling? how many occurrences do i have for taxa that are at the infraspecific level?

```{julia}
#filter data frame for matches at specificEpithet
gdf = groupby(taxa_traits, :specificEpithet)
duplicated_specifictaxa = filter(g -> nrow(g) > 1, gdf) |> DataFrame
ismissing(duplicated_specifictaxa) #false
```

No, we don't have any instances of this. good!
